top_categories.workout_context: When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
At mile 60 of 93, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Gran Fondo Mont-Tremblant's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before August.
Gran Fondo Mont-Tremblant's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Gran Fondo Mont-Tremblant. Train it until it feels boring.
Gran Fondo Mont-Tremblant's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 93 miles.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 93 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Gran Fondo Mont-Tremblant's mixed terrain actually does to you.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 93 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Tempo is Gran Fondo Mont-Tremblant's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 93 miles on mixed terrain. Train it until it's automatic.
At Gran Fondo Mont-Tremblant, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mixed terrain at tempo for 93 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Gran Fondo Mont-Tremblant’s 93 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Gran Fondo Mont-Tremblant. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Durability, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 93 miles of mixed terrain in Mont-Tremblant, Quebec, Canada.
